definitions: To leave a place or situation, often abruptly or in protest.
To get rid of something, especially an unpleasant feeling or physical effect, by walking.
To win a game or competition, especially in a dramatic fashion, by scoring the winning point or run.
To steal something, especially by casually taking it.
examples: She decided to walk off the job.
He tried to walk off his anger.
The team walked off with the championship.
Someone walked off with my umbrella.
